content.claim: Finland's comprehensive reform (1960s–70s) replaced a dual-track system with 9 years of untracked basic education to age 16, achieving high mean performance and low between-school variance in early PISA cycles (2000–2009) attributed to egalitarian design.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: PISA outcomes are population-level descriptive facts; causal attribution to de-tracking alone cannot exclude other simultaneous reforms (curriculum, teacher education, funding)
Early period outcomes (2000–2009) do not necessarily predict long-term system stability
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
